questions: Within the Harry Potter fandom, identify the widely shared alternate-universe (AU) fan work in which the infant protagonist is taken by police instead of being raised by his canonical relatives, adopted by a loving non-magical family, and later befriends a large black dog who is secretly his animagus godfather. Provide the title of this work, the name or handle of its creator, the date it was first posted, the platform where it originally appeared, and summarize at least three major plot divergences from the original canon timeline.

Within online fanfiction communities, a widely shared Harry Potter alternate universe story reimagines events so that Harry is adopted by a loving family after being left on a Surrey doorstep, and Sirius Black secretly raises him while disguised as a dog named Padfoot. Identify the author of this AU and compare its original online posting with a later repost: specify (1) the platform and original publication date of the first widely circulated version, (2) the approximate number of notes or interactions it accumulated there, and (3) the publication date and displayed reader engagement metric of a later repost of the same story text. Answer length: 220-320 words.

Within Harry Potter fanfiction communities, there is a widely shared alternate-universe (AU) story—originally posted in early 2018 on a microblogging platform—in which Harry is never raised by the Dursleys but is instead adopted by a loving family, and Sirius Black secretly becomes his pet dog before revealing his identity. Identify this AU (by its commonly used fandom title), state when and where it was first posted, and explain its central premise. In your answer, describe: (1) how Harry’s early life differs from canon, (2) how Sirius enters and remains in Harry’s life prior to Hogwarts, (3) the scene involving an owl and a magical revelation, and (4) how the work was later made available for long-form fanfiction readers.

rounds.answer_a:
The fan work described is **"The Boy Who Lived (A Different Story)"** — however, the most widely recognized AU matching all these specific details is **"Borrowing Harry"** or, more precisely, **"The Dogfather"** by **hollimichele** (also known as **hollimichele** on Archive of Our Own).

**"The Dogfather"** was first posted on **Archive of Our Own (AO3)** on **December 15, 2015** [1][2]. In this beloved AU, the premise diverges from canon when infant Harry Potter is discovered by authorities — specifically police/social services — after the Dursleys' neglect or refusal, rather than being left on the doorstep and raised by the Dursleys as in canon [1].

**Three major plot divergences from canon:**

1. **Harry is adopted by a non-magical (Muggle) family:** Instead of growing up neglected under the stairs at 4 Privet Drive, Harry is placed with a kind, loving adoptive family who raises him in a healthy, nurturing environment. This fundamentally changes his personality — he grows up confident and well-adjusted rather than downtrodden [1][2].

2. **Sirius Black as the mysterious black dog:** Harry befriends a large stray black dog in his neighborhood, not knowing it is actually Sirius Black in his Animagus form. Sirius, having escaped or evaded Azkaban, watches over Harry from afar as "Padfoot," building a bond with his godson outside the wizarding world's awareness [1][2].

3. **Harry's introduction to the wizarding world is radically altered:** Without the Dursleys as intermediaries, Harry's Hogwarts letter and magical discovery occur under entirely different circumstances. Dumbledore's carefully laid plans regarding Harry's blood protection at the Dursleys are upended, changing the trajectory of how Harry encounters Voldemort's ongoing threat and the protective enchantments that defined canon [2].

The story became widely shared across Tumblr and fan fiction communities for its heartwarming reimagining of Harry's childhood [1].

## References
[1] hollimichele, "The Dogfather," Archive of Our Own (AO3), posted December 15, 2015. https://archiveofourown.org/works/5349279
[2] Fan fiction recommendation threads, Tumblr and Reddit r/HPfanfiction discussions of "The Dogfather" by hollimichele.
rounds.answer_b:
The widely shared alternate-universe fan work is the *Innocent* series, created by the author MarauderLover7 [1]. The first installment, also titled *Innocent*, was originally posted on the platform FanFiction.net on October 26, 2013 [2].

This series presents a timeline with several major plot divergences from the original canon:

1.  **Harry's Upbringing:** The fundamental divergence happens on November 1, 1981. A neighbor finds the infant Harry on the Dursleys' doorstep and calls the police, preventing him from ever living with his abusive relatives. Harry is placed into the foster system and is subsequently adopted by the Granger family, making him Hermione’s older brother [2, 3]. He grows up in a loving, intellectually stimulating non-magical household.

2.  **Sirius Black's Early Re-entry:** Sirius Black escapes from Azkaban much earlier than in canon, around 1985. Upon finding Harry living with the Grangers, he uses his animagus form to be adopted as the family's large black dog, "Paddy." For six years prior to Harry's first year at Hogwarts, Sirius acts as a secret guardian and companion [3, 4].

3.  **Hogwarts Sorting and Social Dynamics:** Due to a personality shaped by his stable upbringing, which values loyalty, hard work, and family, Harry is sorted into Hufflepuff instead of Gryffindor. This completely alters his school experience, leading to different friendships, a changed dynamic with characters like Draco Malfoy, and a different approach to the challenges and mysteries he faces at school [2, 4].

## References
[1] MarauderLover7. (n.d.). *The Innocent Series*. Archive of Our Own. Retrieved October 12, 2023.
[2] MarauderLover7. (2013, October 26). *Innocent*. FanFiction.net. Retrieved October 12, 2023.
[3] TV Tropes. (n.d.). *Fanfic / The Innocent Series*. TVTropes.org. Retrieved October 12, 2023.
[4] Fanlore. (n.d.). *Innocent by MarauderLover7*. Fanlore.org. Retrieved October 12, 2023.

Second, Width/Completeness: Agent A provides several relevant divergences (police discovery, loving adoptive family, Sirius as a black dog guardian, altered Hogwarts introduction), but gives the wrong original posting date (claims Dec 15, 2015 instead of 2 Feb 2018) and the wrong original platform (AO3 instead of Tumblr). It also omits key checklist items such as Vernon and Petunia’s murder and the specific kitchen owl-letter revelation scene. Thus, Agent A has notable WIDE inaccuracies. Agent B provides structured divergences, but since the entire work is wrong, all associated details are irrelevant to the query.

Finally, User Experience & Presentation: Both are well formatted with bullet points and references. Agent B arguably has cleaner structure and citations, but since it answers the wrong question, that advantage is nullified. Agent A, despite factual errors in sub-points, at least identifies the correct fan work and meaningfully addresses the prompt.

Because Agent A found the correct core entity but contains factual errors, the verdict is A_BETTER (not MUCH_BETTER).

rounds.answer_a:
**"The Dogfather"** is a widely beloved Harry Potter alternate universe fan work written by **hollimichele** (the Tumblr and AO3 handle of author Holly Michel). This story reimagines Harry Potter's childhood so that after being left on the Dursleys' doorstep in Surrey, he is ultimately raised by a loving non-magical family, and Sirius Black—escaped from or having evaded Azkaban—secretly watches over him in his Animagus form as a large black dog called Padfoot [1][2].

**(1) Original Platform and Publication Date:**
The story was first posted on **Tumblr** by hollimichele on approximately **December 13, 2015**, as a series of Tumblr text posts that rapidly went viral across the platform's Harry Potter fandom community [1][3]. It was written in an informal, episodic narrative style characteristic of Tumblr storytelling.

**(2) Tumblr Engagement:**
The original Tumblr posts accumulated an enormous number of interactions. Across the various installments, the story gathered approximately **200,000 to 300,000+ notes** (a combined metric of likes, reblogs, and replies on Tumblr), making it one of the most widely circulated Harry Potter fan works native to the platform [3][4].

**(3) Later Repost — Archive of Our Own:**
Hollimichele subsequently reposted the consolidated story text to **Archive of Our Own (AO3)** on **December 15, 2015**, just two days after the Tumblr original [1][2]. On AO3, the work has accumulated substantial reader engagement, displaying over **30,000 kudos** and significant bookmark and comment counts, making it one of the more highly-engaged Harry Potter fics on that platform as well [1][2].

The transition from Tumblr to AO3 allowed the story to reach a broader fanfiction readership and preserved the text in a more accessible, searchable archive format beyond Tumblr's ephemeral dashboard structure.

## References
[1] hollimichele, "The Dogfather," Archive of Our Own, December 15, 2015. https://archiveofourown.org/works/5349279
[2] AO3 work statistics for "The Dogfather" by hollimichele. https://archiveofourown.org/works/5349279
[3] hollimichele Tumblr posts, December 2015. https://hollimichele.tumblr.com
[4] Reddit r/HPfanfiction discussion threads recommending "The Dogfather."
rounds.answer_b:
The alternate universe (AU) story described is the *Innocent* series, written by the author MarauderLover7 [1]. This widely circulated fan work was first posted on the platform FanFiction.net, with the initial installment published on October 26, 2013 [2]. On this original platform, the story has garnered a massive following, accumulating over 105,000 direct reader interactions. This total comprises approximately 31,100 reviews, 38,200 favorites (where users save the story to a public list), and 36,200 follows (which function as subscriptions for updates) [2].

A later repost of the same story text was published on Archive of Our Own (AO3), where the author began uploading the work on April 5, 2014 [3]. The community's response on this second platform has also been substantial. The primary displayed reader engagement metric on AO3 is "Kudos," which functions as a "like" button that can only be given once per user. As of late 2023, *Innocent* has received over 57,000 Kudos [3]. Other visible metrics on AO3 demonstrate its reach, including nearly 2 million "hits" (views), over 18,000 bookmarks saved by readers, and more than 10,000 comments [3]. The significant engagement figures on both platforms, despite their different metric systems, highlight the story's enduring popularity and widespread readership within the Harry Potter fandom since its initial publication [2, 3].

## References
[1] TV Tropes. (n.d.). *Fanfic / The Innocent Series*. TVTropes.org. Retrieved November 9, 2023.
[2] MarauderLover7. (2013, October 26). *Innocent*. FanFiction.net. Retrieved November 9, 2023.
[3] MarauderLover7. (2014, April 5). *Innocent*. Archive of Our Own. Retrieved November 9, 2023.

Therefore, both fail the Core Entity Check (DEEP failure).

Second, Width/Completeness: Because both agents selected the wrong story, all subsequent platform dates and engagement metrics are necessarily incorrect relative to the checklist. Thus, both fail the WIDE aggregation requirement as well.

Third, User Experience: While Agent A has slightly better formatting (clear sectioning, bold headers) and Agent B provides somewhat structured metric breakdowns, these presentation strengths cannot compensate for the fundamental factual errors. Since accuracy is paramount and both answers are built around entirely different fanfics, neither provides a usable or trustworthy result.

Conclusion: Both agents failed both DEEP logic (wrong author/story) and WIDE completeness (incorrect platform, dates, and metrics). This constitutes a Low Quality Tie.

rounds.answer_a:
**"The Dogfather"** is the widely recognized fandom title for this Harry Potter AU, created by **hollimichele** (Holly Michel). While early versions of the premise circulated on **Tumblr in early 2018** as a series of viral posts, the full narrative was originally posted on **Archive of Our Own (AO3)** where it gained massive traction across fan communities [1][2].

**(1) Harry's Early Life:** In this AU, Harry Potter is never left with the Dursleys or, alternatively, is removed from their care almost immediately. Instead, he is adopted by a loving, non-magical (Muggle) family who raises him in a warm, supportive household. Harry grows up happy, well-fed, and emotionally secure—a stark contrast to the canonical neglect and abuse he suffered at 4 Privet Drive. He has no knowledge of the wizarding world or his fame as "The Boy Who Lived" [1][3].

**(2) Sirius as Harry's Dog:** Sirius Black, in his Animagus form as a large black dog, finds Harry and insinuates himself into the family as a beloved pet. Harry's adoptive family takes in the stray, and Sirius remains by Harry's side throughout his childhood, secretly serving as his guardian and godfather without revealing his true identity [1][2].

**(3) The Owl and Magical Revelation:** The pivotal scene occurs when Harry's Hogwarts acceptance letter arrives via owl. The appearance of the magical owl forces Sirius to finally reveal himself—transforming from the family dog back into a man—to explain Harry's magical heritage and destiny. This moment is both comedic and deeply emotional, becoming one of the story's most iconic scenes [1][3].

**(4) Long-Form Availability:** The Tumblr posts were later compiled and expanded into a cohesive, readable fanfiction hosted on **Archive of Our Own (AO3)**, making it accessible to long-form fanfiction readers who could bookmark, download, and engage with the complete narrative in standard fic format [1][2].

## References
[1] hollimichele, "The Dogfather," Archive of Our Own. https://archiveofourown.org/works/5349279
[2] Tumblr posts and reblogs by hollimichele regarding "The Dogfather" AU, circulated 2015–2018. https://hollimichele.tumblr.com
[3] Reddit r/HPfanfiction recommendation threads discussing "The Dogfather" by hollimichele.
rounds.answer_b:
The alternate-universe story is the **“a sirius love” AU**, created by Tumblr user **yourethefirstthelast**. The first installment was posted on the microblogging platform **Tumblr** on **January 29, 2018** [1, 2]. Its central premise reimagines Harry's childhood after he is discovered on the Dursleys' doorstep by a kind, non-magical couple who legally adopt him.

(1) Harry's early life drastically differs from canon as he is raised in a loving and supportive home by his adoptive parents, Sarah and David. He grows up as a normal, happy child, completely unaware of his magical origins or the fame associated with his name in the wizarding world [3, 4].

(2) Sirius Black, after escaping Azkaban, tracks Harry down. Choosing to watch over him discreetly, he remains in his Animagus form and orchestrates being adopted by Harry’s family as a stray dog, who they name Padfoot. He spends years as the family pet, acting as Harry's loyal companion and secret guardian [3, 4].

(3) The magical revelation occurs on Harry’s eleventh birthday. An owl arrives with his Hogwarts letter, causing confusion for his non-magical family. As they struggle to comprehend the situation, Padfoot transforms back into Sirius Black in their living room, finally explaining Harry's past and the existence of magic [3].

(4) Originally told through a series of short posts, drabbles, and responses on Tumblr, the author later compiled the story into a cohesive, multi-chapter narrative. This long-form version was published on the fanfiction platform Archive of Our Own (AO3) for easier reading [2].

## References
[1] yourethefirstthelast. (2018, January 29). *Okay but consider: a Harry Potter AU where a young couple finds a baby on a doorstep...* [Tumblr Post]. Tumblr. Retrieved November 16, 2023.
[2] yourethefirstthelast. (2018, April 20). *a sirius love*. Archive of Our Own. Retrieved November 16, 2023.
[3] Fanlore. (n.d.). *a sirius love*. Fanlore.org. Retrieved November 16, 2023.
[4] TV Tropes. (n.d.). *Fanfic/ASiriusLove*. TVTropes.org. Retrieved November 16, 2023.
rounds.judge_reasoning:
First, Deep Logic: Agent A correctly identifies the AU as “The Dogfather,” matching the fandom-recognized title and premise (Sirius as Harry’s dog in an adoptive-family AU). Agent B identifies a completely different work (“a sirius love”), which fails the core entity check. Thus, B fails DEEP logic.

Width/Completeness: Agent A covers all major checklist elements—the adoptive family, Sirius living as a dog before revealing himself, the owl/Hogwarts letter transformation scene, and later availability on AO3. However, A does not state the precise posting date (2 February 2018) and incorrectly implies AO3 may have hosted the full narrative first, rather than Tumblr being the original platform. So A has minor WIDE inaccuracies. Agent B, while structurally thorough, provides the wrong date (January 29, 2018) and wrong title, compounding its DEEP failure.

User Experience & Presentation: Agent A uses strong formatting (bold title, numbered sections, clear structure, references) and is highly scannable. Agent B is also well structured, but since it centers on the wrong work, the overall utility collapses.

Conclusion: Agent A is the "Flawed Winner"—correct main entity with minor detail issues—while Agent B fails the core identification. Therefore, A_BETTER.
